tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The two f2p tests (test_hmr_data_initialization_in_template and test_initialization_inside_if_block) are purely text-matching: they open the file and assert string/regex patterns. They do not import, call, or execute the code. The p2p tests do run subprocesses (build, unit tests, etc.), but the f2p tests are entirely 'assert string in open(path).read()' style.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check for the presence of the literal string 'import.meta.hot.data ??= {}' and a regex pattern in the source file. An agent could simply append a comment containing that string to the file (e.g., '// import.meta.hot.data ??= {}') and pass test_hmr_data_initialization_in_template without implementing the actual fix. The regex test is slightly harder to fool but still just checks text presence.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The agent can read /workspace/router and the solution directory if it exists. More critically, the instruction.md itself literally states the exact string to insert ('import.meta.hot.data ??= {}') and the tests just check for that literal string in the file, so the agent can grep for or directly use the fixture value without understanding the bug.
